title: Eastern European Time

[Armenia Time](/wiki/Armenia_Time "Armenia Time") / [Azerbaijan Time](/wiki/Azerbaijan_Time "Azerbaijan Time") / [Georgia Time](/wiki/Georgia_Time "Georgia Time") / [Samara Time](/wiki/Samara_Time "Samara Time") ([UTC+4](/wiki/UTC%2B04:00 "UTC+04:00"))

* [Central European Time](/wiki/Central_European_Time "Central European Time") * [West Africa Time](/wiki/West_Africa_Time "West Africa Time") ([UTC+1](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00"))[[b]](#endnote_template_footnote_b)

* [Central Africa Time](/wiki/Central_Africa_Time "Central Africa Time") * Eastern European Time * [South African Standard Time](/wiki/South_African_Standard_Time "South African Standard Time") ([UTC+2](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00"))

infobox.key: [Egypt Summer Time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time_in_Egypt "Daylight saving time in Egypt") ([UTC+3](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00"))

infobox.value: * [Eastern European Summer Time](/wiki/Eastern_European_Summer_Time "Eastern European Summer Time") * [Israel Summer Time](/wiki/Israel_Summer_Time "Israel Summer Time") * [Palestine Summer Time](/wiki/Palestine_Summer_Time "Palestine Summer Time")

(**EET**) is one of the names of the [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00") [time zone](/wiki/Time_zone "Time zone"), 2 hours ahead of [Coordinated Universal Time](/wiki/Coordinated_Universal_Time "Coordinated Universal Time"). The zone uses [daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time"), so that it uses [UTC+03:00](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00") during the summer.

(for Finland, Lithuania, Estonia,[[8]](#cite_note-8) or sometimes Finland, Latvia, Estonia[[9]](#cite_note-9)) or **GTB Standard Time** (for Greece, Turkey, Bulgaria) is used to refer to Eastern European Time.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Time zone in Eastern Europe (GMT +2)

This article is about the time zone with daylight change in Europe. For the static time zone, see [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00").

[Standard time](/wiki/Standard_time "Standard time") observed all year

[Daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time") observed

**Eastern European Time** (**EET**) is one of the names of the [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00") [time zone](/wiki/Time_zone "Time zone"), 2 hours ahead of [Coordinated Universal Time](/wiki/Coordinated_Universal_Time "Coordinated Universal Time"). The zone uses [daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time"), so that it uses [UTC+03:00](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00") during the summer.

A number of African countries use [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00") all year long, where it is called [Central Africa Time](/wiki/Central_Africa_Time "Central Africa Time") (CAT),[[1]](#cite_note-1) although [Egypt](/wiki/Egypt "Egypt") and [Libya](/wiki/Libya "Libya") also use the term *Eastern European Time*.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The most populous city in the Eastern European Time zone is [Cairo](/wiki/Cairo "Cairo"), with the most populous EET city in Europe being [Kyiv](/wiki/Kyiv "Kyiv").

## Usage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eastern_European_Time&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Usage")]

The following countries, parts of countries, and territories use **Eastern European Time** all year round:

* [Kaliningrad Oblast](/wiki/Kaliningrad_Oblast "Kaliningrad Oblast") ([Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia")), since 26 October 2014; also used EET in the years 1945 and 1991–2011. See also [Kaliningrad Time](/wiki/Kaliningrad_Time "Kaliningrad Time").
* [Libya](/wiki/Libya "Libya"), since 27 October 2013; switched from [Central European Time](/wiki/Central_European_Time "Central European Time"), which was used in 2012. Used year-round EET from 1980 to 1981, 1990–1996 and 1998–2012.

The following countries, parts of countries, and territories use **Eastern European Time** during the winter only:

* [Bulgaria](/wiki/Bulgaria "Bulgaria"), since 1894, except between 1942 and 1945
* [Cyprus](/wiki/Cyprus "Cyprus")
* [Egypt](/wiki/Egypt "Egypt"), in the years 1988–2010, 2014–2015 and since 2023 (see also [Egypt Standard Time](/wiki/Egypt_Standard_Time "Egypt Standard Time"))
* [Estonia](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia"), in the years 1921–40 and since 1990
* [Finland](/wiki/Finland "Finland"), since 1921
* [Greece](/wiki/Greece "Greece"), since 1916
* [Israel](/wiki/Israel "Israel"), since 1948 (see also [Israel Standard Time](/wiki/Israel_Standard_Time "Israel Standard Time"))
* [Latvia](/wiki/Latvia "Latvia"), in the years 1926–40 and since 1990
* [Lebanon](/wiki/Lebanon "Lebanon")
* [Lithuania](/wiki/Lithuania "Lithuania"), in 1920–40 and since 1990 with a break 1998–1999
* [Moldova](/wiki/Moldova "Moldova"), in the years 1918–40, 1941–44 and since 1991
  + Including [Transnistria](/wiki/Transnistria "Transnistria")
* [Palestine](/wiki/Palestine "Palestine") (see also [Palestine Standard Time](/wiki/Time_in_the_State_of_Palestine "Time in the State of Palestine"))
* [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania")
* [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine"), in the years 1922–30 and since 1990[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The following countries, parts of countries, and territories used **Eastern European Time** in the past:

* [Moscow](/wiki/Moscow_Time "Moscow Time") used EET in the years 1922–30 and 1991–92.
* [Belarus](/wiki/Belarus "Belarus"), in the years 1922–30 and 1990–2011[[4]](#cite_note-4)
* [Jordan](/wiki/Jordan "Jordan") used EET until permanently switching to DST in 2022.[[5]](#cite_note-jo-5)
* In [Poland](/wiki/Second_Polish_Republic "Second Polish Republic"), this time was used in the years 1919–22.
* [Crimea](/wiki/Crimea "Crimea") used EET as part of Ukraine between 1991–1994 and 1996–2014 and started using [Moscow Time](/wiki/Moscow_Time "Moscow Time") because of the [annexation of Crimea by the Russian Federation](/wiki/Annexation_of_Crimea_by_the_Russian_Federation "Annexation of Crimea by the Russian Federation") in 2014.
* [Syria](/wiki/Syria "Syria") used EET until permanently switching to DST in 2022.[[5]](#cite_note-jo-5)
* [Turkey](/wiki/Turkey "Turkey"), used EET in the years 1910–1978 and re-used it again in the years 1985–2016.[[6]](#cite_note-time_Time-6)

Sometimes, because it is used on [Microsoft Windows](/wiki/Microsoft_Windows "Microsoft Windows"),[[7]](#cite_note-7) **FLE Standard Time** (for Finland, Lithuania, Estonia,[[8]](#cite_note-8) or sometimes Finland, Latvia, Estonia[[9]](#cite_note-9)) or **GTB Standard Time** (for Greece, Turkey, Bulgaria) is used to refer to Eastern European Time.

## Anomalies

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eastern_European_Time&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Anomalies")]

Since political, in addition to purely geographical, criteria are used in the drawing of time zones, it follows that time zones do not precisely adhere to meridian lines. The EET ([UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00")) time zone, were it drawn by purely geographical terms, would consist of exactly the area between meridians 22°30' E and 37°30' E. As a result, there are European locales that despite lying in an area with a "physical" [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00") time, are in another time zone; likewise, there are European areas that have gone for [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00"), even though their "physical" time zone is different from that. Following is a list of such anomalies:

### Areas outside [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00") longitudes using Eastern European Time ([UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00")) time

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eastern_European_Time&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Areas outside UTC+02:00 longitudes using Eastern European Time (UTC+02:00) time")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/09/Tzdiff-Europe-winter.png/330px-Tzdiff-Europe-winter.png)](/wiki/File:Tzdiff-Europe-winter.png)

| Colour | Legal time vs local mean time |
| --- | --- |
|  | 1 h ± 30 m behind |
|  | 0 h ± 30 m |
|  | 1 h ± 30 m ahead |
|  | 2 h ± 30 m ahead |
|  | 3 h ± 30 m ahead |

#### Areas west of 22°30' E ("physical" [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00")) that use [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00")

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eastern_European_Time&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Areas west of 22°30' E (\"physical\" UTC+01:00) that use UTC+02:00")]

* The westernmost part of Greece, including the cities of [Patras](/wiki/Patras "Patras") and [Ioannina](/wiki/Ioannina "Ioannina"), and the [Ionian Islands](/wiki/Ionian_Islands_(region) "Ionian Islands (region)")
* The very westernmost parts of the [Bulgarian](/wiki/Bulgaria "Bulgaria") provinces of [Vidin](/wiki/Vidin "Vidin") and [Kyustendil](/wiki/Kyustendil "Kyustendil")
* The westernmost part of [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"), including most of the area of the counties of [Caraș-Severin](/wiki/Cara%C8%99-Severin_County "Caraș-Severin County"), [Timiș](/wiki/Timi%C8%99_County "Timiș County") (capital [Timișoara](/wiki/Timi%C8%99oara "Timișoara")), [Arad](/wiki/Arad_County "Arad County"), and [Bihor](/wiki/Bihor_County "Bihor County"), as well as the westernmost tips of the counties of [Mehedinți](/wiki/Mehedin%C8%9Bi_County "Mehedinți County") and [Satu Mare](/wiki/Satu_Mare_County "Satu Mare County")
* The extreme westernmost tip of [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine"), near the border with Hungary and [Slovakia](/wiki/Slovakia "Slovakia"), at the Ukrainian [Transcarpathian Oblast](/wiki/Transcarpathian_Oblast "Transcarpathian Oblast") ([Zakarpattia Oblast](/wiki/Zakarpattia_Oblast "Zakarpattia Oblast")), essentially comprising the city of [Uzhhorod](/wiki/Uzhhorod "Uzhhorod") and its environs
* Most of the [Kaliningrad Oblast](/wiki/Kaliningrad_Oblast "Kaliningrad Oblast"), including the cities of [Kaliningrad](/wiki/Kaliningrad "Kaliningrad") and [Baltiysk](/wiki/Baltiysk "Baltiysk")
* Western [Lithuania](/wiki/Lithuania "Lithuania"), including the cities of [Klaipėda](/wiki/Klaip%C4%97da "Klaipėda"), [Tauragė](/wiki/Taurag%C4%97 "Tauragė"), and [Telšiai](/wiki/Tel%C5%A1iai "Telšiai")
* Western [Latvia](/wiki/Latvia "Latvia"), including the cities of [Liepāja](/wiki/Liep%C4%81ja "Liepāja") and [Ventspils](/wiki/Ventspils "Ventspils")
* The westernmost parts of the [Estonian](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia") islands of [Saaremaa](/wiki/Saaremaa "Saaremaa") and [Hiiumaa](/wiki/Hiiumaa "Hiiumaa"), including the capital of the [Saare County](/wiki/Saare_County "Saare County"), [Kuressaare](/wiki/Kuressaare "Kuressaare")
* The southwestern coast of [Finland](/wiki/Finland "Finland"), including the cities of [Turku](/wiki/Turku "Turku"), [Pori](/wiki/Pori "Pori"), and [Vaasa](/wiki/Vaasa "Vaasa"); also the [Åland](/wiki/%C3%85land "Åland") islands (of Finnish jurisdiction) – the [Åland](/wiki/%C3%85land "Åland") islands are the westernmost locale applying EET in the whole of Europe
* Most of [Libya](/wiki/Libya "Libya") including the capital [Tripoli](/wiki/Tripoli,_Libya "Tripoli, Libya")
* The western half of the municipality of [Enontekiö](/wiki/Enonteki%C3%B6 "Enontekiö") in northwestern [Finland](/wiki/Finland "Finland")

**Areas east of 37°30' E ("physical" [UTC+03:00](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00")) that use [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00")**

* Eastern parts of [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine"), including the city of [Mariupol](/wiki/Mariupol "Mariupol")

### Areas within [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00") longitudes (22°30' E – 37°30' E) using other time zones

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eastern_European_Time&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Areas within UTC+02:00 longitudes (22°30' E – 37°30' E) using other time zones")]

#### Areas that use [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00")

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eastern_European_Time&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Areas that use UTC+01:00")]

These areas have sunrises and sunsets at least half an hour earlier than places on the [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00") meridian.

* The easternmost part of [North Macedonia](/wiki/North_Macedonia "North Macedonia"), including the city of [Strumica](/wiki/Strumica "Strumica").
* The absolutely easternmost part of [Serbia](/wiki/Serbia "Serbia"), in the [Pirot District](/wiki/Pirot_District "Pirot District"), including the city of [Pirot](/wiki/Pirot "Pirot").
* The extreme easternmost tips of Hungary and [Slovakia](/wiki/Slovakia "Slovakia"), bordering to the north and south respectively the Ukrainian [Transcarpathian Oblast](/wiki/Transcarpathian_Oblast "Transcarpathian Oblast") ([Zakarpattia Oblast](/wiki/Zakarpattia_Oblast "Zakarpattia Oblast")), a bit to the east of the [Vásárosnamény](/wiki/V%C3%A1s%C3%A1rosnam%C3%A9ny "Vásárosnamény"), Hungary – [Uzhhorod](/wiki/Uzhhorod "Uzhhorod"), [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine") (both at 22°18' E) line
* The easternmost part of Poland, including the cities of [Lublin](/wiki/Lublin "Lublin") and [Białystok](/wiki/Bia%C5%82ystok "Białystok")
* The extreme northeast of Sweden, in the [Norrbotten province](/wiki/Norrbotten "Norrbotten"), including the cities of [Kalix](/wiki/Kalix "Kalix") and [Haparanda](/wiki/Haparanda "Haparanda")
* The northeast of Norway, lying north of Finland, roughly coinciding with the county of [Finnmark](/wiki/Finnmark "Finnmark"). The easternmost town in Norway, [Vardø](/wiki/Vard%C3%B8_(town) "Vardø (town)"), lies at 30°51' E, which is east of the central meridian of [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00"), i.e. east of [Istanbul](/wiki/Istanbul "Istanbul") and [Alexandria](/wiki/Alexandria "Alexandria"). The [Norwegian-Russian border](/wiki/Norwegian-Russian_border "Norwegian-Russian border") and [Belarus–Poland border](/wiki/Belarus%E2%80%93Poland_border "Belarus–Poland border") are the only places where CET (UTC+1) borders Moscow time ([UTC+03:00](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00")), resulting in a one (or two in winter) hour time change when crossing that border. There is a "tri-zone" point (where [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00"), [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00"), and [UTC+03:00](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00") meet) at the [Norway-Finland-Russia tripoint](/wiki/Muotkavaara "Muotkavaara"), near the town of [Rayakoski](/wiki/Rayakoski "Rayakoski").

#### Areas that use [UTC+03:00](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00")

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eastern_European_Time&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Areas that use UTC+03:00")]

Most of the following countries use winter time year round, so they coincide with Eastern European Summer Time in summer.

* [Belarus](/wiki/Belarus "Belarus") is between 23°11′E and 32°47′E and is thus fully within the physical [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00") area, but it uses [UTC+03:00](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00") year around.
* Practically all [European Russia](/wiki/European_Russia "European Russia") west of Moscow (except [Kaliningrad Oblast](/wiki/Kaliningrad_Oblast "Kaliningrad Oblast")); this includes the chunk of land from [Murmansk](/wiki/Murmansk "Murmansk") all the way south to [Belgorod](/wiki/Belgorod "Belgorod"), including the cities of [St. Petersburg](/wiki/St._Petersburg "St. Petersburg"), [Novgorod](/wiki/Novgorod "Novgorod"), and [Pskov](/wiki/Pskov "Pskov"), to name only a few. (The westernmost point of contiguous Russia, near [Lavry](/wiki/Lavry "Lavry"), [Pskov Oblast](/wiki/Pskov_Oblast "Pskov Oblast"), 27°19' E, is the westernmost point in European Russia where [UTC+03:00](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00") is applied.) This also includes the city of [Anapa](/wiki/Anapa "Anapa"), at the westernmost tip of the [Krasnodar Krai](/wiki/Krasnodar_Krai "Krasnodar Krai") near the entrance to the Sea of Azov, at 37°22' E.
* Parts of western [Syria](/wiki/Syria "Syria"), including the capital [Damascus](/wiki/Damascus "Damascus")
* Most of [Jordan](/wiki/Jordan "Jordan"), including the capital [Amman](/wiki/Amman "Amman")
* Western half of [Turkey](/wiki/Turkey "Turkey"), including the capital [Ankara](/wiki/Ankara "Ankara") and the most populous city [Istanbul](/wiki/Istanbul "Istanbul")

### Tripoints and borders between zones

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eastern_European_Time&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Tripoints and borders between zones")]

* The Norway–Russia–Finland "tri-zone" point at [Muotkavaara](/wiki/Muotkavaara "Muotkavaara") (see [Central European Time](/wiki/Central_European_Time "Central European Time")) is surrounded by three different times in winter, two in summer. It had three time zones year-around before 2014.
* Two of the four tripoints of [Belarus](/wiki/Belarus "Belarus") and the tripoint of the [Kaliningrad Region](/wiki/Kaliningrad_Oblast "Kaliningrad Oblast") are surrounded by three different times in winter.
